action_items.action: NEW — GDCU-8878 🔥 CA/FR Oplist Redesign moved to In Product (Kelvin Yee, 13:19 today). Was In QA this morning. Kelvin has implemented the full oplist redesign for gambling.com/ca/fr/casinos-en-ligne per the AI Day prototype. Acceptance criteria: editor picks = top 3 brands, scrollable T&Cs, comparison + show-more functions, all device/browser checks. Stakeholders: Gabriel, Eoin, Ciara, Joshua, Sarah Azzopardi, Diana Thurmann. Review and approve or feedback today.

NEW — GDCU-8936 Ace Navigation Redesign → In Product (Carlos Blauth, 11:54 today). NOT in morning report. Carlos has implemented the full redesign of Ace assistant navigation with global nav bar integration: new burger menu (right-side, left sidebar), updated user icon, sign-in/sign-up redesign with icons on left, new sidebar toggle icon. Stakeholders: Ciara, Chloe, Joshua, Sanja, Shane, Mike, Miguel, Eoin. Review and approve — significant UX change to the Ace assistant.

NEW — GDCU-8955 Cool Tones Phase 2 → In Product (Harden Thomas Wallace, 11:55 today). Was In QA this morning. Harden has implemented Jessica's Phase 2 feedback: FAQ section padding/weight, casino nav white background + border-radius, article card styling, gray table correction, red link consistency, comments padding. Mike named as stakeholder. Review and approve or provide feedback.

NEW — GDCU-8847 VIP ktag Operator List → In Product (Nebojsa Stanic, 11:48 today). Was In QA this morning. Nebojsa has implemented the rev-share operator list for VIP users (ktag VIP level ≥ 2) on UK. Uses AdTech personalisation API with g_uuid, falls back to D1 database. Acceptance: VIP see custom list, non-VIP see regular list, filters and show-more work. Stakeholders: Gabriel, Chloe, Joshua. Review and approve.

CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8874 Page Bloat: Reduce HTML payload size (Harden Thomas Wallace, HIGH, In Product). Updated 07/05 14:30 — unchanged since morning. SEO-critical: uk/online-casinos page is 3,789 KB / 29,300 DOM nodes. Four prioritised fixes: (1) CookieYes lazy render, (2) SSR operator list, (3) VWO async, (4) inline scripts. Mike needs to review approach and confirm implementation order before Harden proceeds. Stakeholders: Chloe, Joshua, Gabriel, Dan Smullen.

CARRY FORWARD — REVIEW GDCU-8806 In-App Age Gate 18+ (Carlos Blauth, MEDIUM, In Product). Unchanged since morning. Apple App Store Guideline 5.3 compliance gate item. Carlos has built age confirmation on first launch, stores preference locally, blocks under-18s. App store submission is gated on Mike's approval. Carlos in progress since 30/03.

CARRY FORWARD — GROUP BETS DESIGN: No update seen since morning. Thursday 07/05 was the target for completion. Confirm with Miguel: is design done? If yes → schedule KO call with devs (as agreed with Chloe on 05/05). If not → assess impact on 11 June hard deadline and update Chloe Christie. Also confirm Karl check-in on WC fixture/resulting data readiness.

NEW — GDCU-8432 🔥 Geo Locate Images Global HP: Ciara posted in #gdc-pod at 13:26 asking whether to use standalone responsive HTML or GIF files (2 GIFs: mobile + desktop) for the geo section. Gabriel replied at 13:36 about not wanting that section crawlable by Google, leaning toward GIF. Decision needed from Mike/dev: confirm HTML vs GIF approach so Ciara can proceed. HIGH priority ticket across 10+ geos (IT, DE, RO, GR, NL, BE, BE/FR, DK, SE, ES, MX).

CARRY FORWARD — UNBLOCK GDCU-8810: Set Up Organisation Level Developer Accounts for Apple/Google (Harden Thomas Wallace, BLOCKED). No update since morning. Prerequisite for app store submission. Identify who holds org-level Apple Developer/Google Play Console credentials and escalate. Harden cannot proceed without them.

CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8907 Betting Tips Hub (Sanja Budic, In QA, priority Blocked). Sanja said 'This should be fixed now' yesterday after Gabriel confirmed European leagues. Status still shows Blocked as of today. Confirm with Sanja whether the fix is live and QA can proceed — if so, update ticket status.

UPDATED — VIC DADSON: Signed off until 16 May (confirmed via Colin Brannigan DM 06/05: 'Vic has been signed off until 16th May. Didn't notify me or HR, just uploaded the doctor note into Bob.'). Morning report assumed return was 05/05 — this updates that context. No immediate action needed this week. Plan re-onboarding from 18/05 (Mon): wellbeing first, then Nemanja requirements + Chris BI team knowledge sharing.

FRIDAY 121 WITH COLIN: Mike's DM to Colin on 06/05 flagged 'one to discuss on Friday in the 121 as some of the blockers at the moment are pretty frustrating — projects either get descoped to a point that they are rubbish, or not done at all.' Today is that Friday. Prep agenda: (1) blocked/descoped project frustrations, (2) Vic's extended sick leave to 16 May, (3) World Cup pipeline status, (4) Apple/Google developer accounts escalation.

NEW — WC PREDICTOR SIGN-IN SPEC delivered this morning: Mike gave Jessica the spec at 09:24 DM — signed-out users can play quizzes but no leaderboards/streaks; IUJ added for sign-in prompt; gate after 3 plays/day requiring sign-in. Jessica confirmed 'Will do and thanks Mike.' Also: Colin/Dean design feedback question closed (Mike: 'Nothing from Colin and Dean'). Design target Tue 12/05 — 4 days. Confirm Miguel has image asset direction for the quiz (Jessica off today).
CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8948 Prize Boxes (Jessica, off today): Awaiting Mike's review of 5th prize box aspect ratio for Cosmo Spins landing page. Review Monday 11/05 when Jessica returns.

RESOLVED ✅ — Duplicate Payment Methods ticket CREATED: GDCU-8959 (Sanja Budic, In Progress, 09:55 today). Covers IS, IS/EN, BE, BE/FR. Morning action item (confirm ticket raised) is now resolved. Track for QA and release.

CARRY FORWARD — INTERVIEW COORDINATION: Liam Caffrey seeking a slot for a candidate interview next Wednesday 14/05 (or Monday 12/05), 12–3pm. Gabriel confirmed Wed works. Check calendar and confirm slot or propose alternative to Liam.

NEW — VERSIVE CREDITS: Mike reminded in #gdc-product-led-growth (09:17) that only half of Versive credits were used last month. Ciara is exploring user-testing Cosmo Spins WC concepts. Ensure team has concrete plans to utilise remaining credits before end of month.

NEW (FYI) — CLAUDE DESIGN CALL: Ciara had a Claude Design System call today with Shiny and Jonathan from Sports team. Built Ace matchmaker ticket as complex example in 1 prompt + 3 error fixes. Ciara also published updated DS in #gdc-product-led-growth (13:29): purple removed from style guide, sports team logos now migrated into DS as components. No action required — awareness only.

CARRY FORWARD — DESIGN SYSTEM GUIDELINES: Team pain point flagged yesterday (Ciara, Miguel, Jessica, Mike) around gradient usage, image standards, Figma assets panel hygiene. Ciara's DS updates today (sports logos, style guide) address some gaps. Consider scheduling a short team alignment once GDCU-8432 (Geo Locate) image direction is confirmed.

needs_input.summary: [GDC][Dev][SEO] Page Bloat: Reduce HTML payload size across all markets — CookieYes, SSR operator list, VWO, inline scripts
needs_input.context: CARRY FORWARD. Updated 07/05 14:30 — unchanged since morning. uk/online-casinos: 3,789 KB / 29,300 DOM nodes (Google limit: 1,500). Four fixes in priority order: (1) CookieYes lazy render saves 432 KB / 18,900 DOM nodes; (2) SSR operator list saves up to 1,212 KB; (3) VWO async saves 379 KB; (4) inline scripts cleanup. Mike needs to confirm implementation order. Stakeholders: Chloe Christie, Joshua Smith, Gabriel Cornoiu, Dan Smullen.
